num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: we construct a comprehensive training set containing 1,888 tool-use dialogues from 2,138 APIs spanning 1,000 distinct domains
This dataset comprises 2,138 distinct APIs and includes 1,888 dialogues with a total of 4,149 API calls, reflecting three different levels of API usage abilities
num_tasks_statement_from_the_paper: we develop a runnable evaluation system consisting of 73 API tools. We annotate 314 tool-use dialogues with 753 API calls
The system comprises 73 commonly used APIs, along with 314 tool-use dialogues containing 753 API calls, all manually annotated
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: We annotate 314 tool-use dialogues with 753 API calls
314 tool-use dialogues containing 753 API calls, all manually annotated
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: we introduce Multi-agent, a novel method using LLMs instead of humans to automatically mass-produce tool-augmented LLM training data

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: ACEBench categorizes data into three primary types based on evaluation methodology: Normal, Special, and Agent
Normal evaluates tool usage in basic scenarios; Special evaluates tool usage in situations with ambiguous or incomplete instructions; Agent evaluates tool usage through multi-agent interactions
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: Special. Includes imperfect instructions, such as instructions containing incomplete parameters, incorrectly formatted parameters
Agent. Encompasses real-world scenarios, abstracted to construct multi-turn, multi-step tool invocation scenarios
has_refusal_ability_statement_from_the_p: Special evaluates tool usage in situations with ambiguous or incomplete instructions
the model's ability to recognize and handle these issues is also crucial for evaluation
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: We build an end-to-end automated evaluation system and develop a sandbox environment construction scheme for multi-turn, multi-step tool invocation
Process Accuracy is based on the consistency between the actual function call process and the ideal function call process

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: we focus on the realm of customer service and create two different domains where agents need to assist simulated users with diverse requests (τ-retail and τ-airline)
focus on the realm of customer service and create two different domains
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: We constructed τ-bench in three stages, including manual schema and API design, LM-assisted generation of data entries, and manual scenario generation and verification for the user simulator
manual schema and API design... and manual scenario generation and verification
has_refusal_ability_statement_from_the_p: An example trajectory in τ-airline, where an agent needs to reject the user request (change a basic economy flight) following domain policies and propose a new solution (cancel and rebook)

The benchmark tests an agent's ability to collate and convey all required information from/to users through multiple interactions, and solve complex issues on the fly while ensuring it follows guidelines
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: diverse user scenarios and corresponding ground truth annotations
Our evaluation scheme compares the database state at the end of each episode with the ground truth expected state

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: A novel Telecom dual-control domain modeled as a Dec-POMDP
we introduce τ²-bench with a dual-control environment
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: A compositional task generator that programmatically creates diverse, verifiable tasks from atomic components, ensuring domain coverage and controlled complexity
compositional task generator that programmatically creates diverse, verifiable tasks

users can take actions and call tools in addition to communicating with the agent
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: programmatically creates diverse, verifiable tasks from atomic components
verifiable tasks from atomic components, ensuring domain coverage and controlled complexity

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: we propose WorkArena, a remote-hosted benchmark of 29 tasks based on the widely-used ServiceNow platform
WorkArena is a benchmark developed on the widely-used ServiceNow platform
num_tasks_statement_from_the_paper: we propose WorkArena, a remote-hosted benchmark of 29 tasks based on the widely-used ServiceNow platform
WorkArena consists of a suite of 29 tasks and 23,150 unique instances that cover core interactions with the ServiceNow platform
has_human_task_curation_statement_from_t: WorkArena consists of a suite of 29 tasks and 23,150 unique instances that cover core interactions with the ServiceNow platform
WorkArena is a realistic benchmark of enterprise-related tasks for web agents comprising 23,150 unique task instances
has_human_plans_statement_from_the_paper: each task can optionally implement a cheat() function, meant to assess the feasibility of the task via a hard-coded Playwright solution
A unique feature of WorkArena in contrast with prior benchmarks is the inclusion of a cheating function for each task, implemented using Playwright browser automation

num_domains_statement_from_the_paper: we propose WorkArena++, a novel benchmark consisting of 682 tasks corresponding to realistic workflows routinely performed by knowledge workers
WorkArena++ is designed to evaluate the planning, problem-solving, logical/arithmetic reasoning, retrieval, and contextual understanding abilities of web agents

consisting of 682 tasks corresponding to realistic workflows

generate thousands of ground-truth observation/action traces, which can be used for fine-tuning
